commit.message:
Implement relaxed versions of `AtomicAccess` members for Windows/AArch64

This patch implements two key changes.  First, prior to this patch, the
Windows/AArch64 implementation of `PlatformAdd`, `PlatformXchg`, and
`PlatformCmpXchg` structs ignored the `atomic_memory_order` parameter
and instead used the strongest barrier semantics for all operations.
This is correct but unnecessarily conservative and also inconsistent
with the Linux and macOS AArch64 ports, both of which dispatch to
different instruction sequences based on the requested ordering.

To bring parity to Windows/AArch64 port, this patch uses different
variants of the MSVC ARM64 compiler intrinsics that support ordering
suffixes.  Specifically, we use the `_nf` variant for no-fence atomics,
the `_acq` variant for acquire atomics, the `_rel` variant for release
atomics, and the variant without suffixes for full barrier atomics.  The
`memory_order_conservative` case uses the full-barrier intrinsic
followed by an explicit `OrderAccess::fence()` for the strongest
possible ordering.

The second change in this patch is to specialize `PlatformOrderedLoad`
and `PlatformOrderedStore` for various sizes and semantics.  In
particular, instead of `PlatformOrderedLoad` emitting `ldr` followed by
`dmb ishld` and `PlatformOrderedStore` emitting `dmb ish` followed by
`str`, both of which are less efficient than the `ldar` and `stlr`
instructions, this patch specializes `PlatformOrderedLoad` and
`PlatformOrderedStore` using MSVC's `__ldar*` and `__stlr*` intrinsics,
which emit single `ldar` and `stlr` instructions.  Doing so avoids
barrier instructions (`dmb`) that stall the pipeline.  The
specialization for `RELEASE_X_FENCE` reuses the specialization for
`RELEASE_X` followed by an explicit LoadStore barrier (`dmb ish`).
